verdict_reasoning: EREG is an EGF-family ligand synthesized as a single-pass transmembrane proligand (proEREG) that is processed by ADAM-family sheddases to release the soluble mature EGF-like domain, which is the dominant signaling actor on EGFR/ERBB4. The TM precursor stage is real and supports juxtacrine signaling but is transient relative to the shed soluble form. Fits dual_localization (transient TM precursor of a shed-ligand-dominant gene) rather than secreted_only.
